title: Helmia furcata
intro: Helmia furcata, also known as Pterosporis, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Helmia furcata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Helmia furcata include tea infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and immune system function.
taxonomy: Helia furcata is classified in the family Rutaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family.
